How It Works
Engineered
for Simplicity
1

Remove the OEM Cap

Pull the stock CV flange cover. The CVease housing threads directly onto the same bolt pattern — no modification required.

2

Bolt On the CVease

The anodized billet housing installs over the joint and seals with the included hardware. Stud-style studs and a Zerk fitting are integrated into the cover.

3

Pump In Fresh Grease

Attach a standard grease gun to the Zerk fitting. Pump until old grease purges through the bleed port — a clear sign the joint is fully packed.

4

Seal and Drive

Cap the bleed port, torque the bolts, and you're done. No grease all over the garage, no wasted time.

About CVease
Built by
Builders

CVease was born from a simple question: Why is greasing a CV joint still such a messy job?

Anyone who has serviced a 930, 934, or virtually any CV joint knows the drill—disassembly, grease-covered hands, a filthy workbench, and a cleanup process that feels longer than the service itself. We knew there had to be a better way.

So we built one!

CVease is designed to deliver fresh grease directly into the joint through a standard Zerk fitting, eliminating the mess, reducing waste, and dramatically simplifying routine maintenance. No more grease where it doesn't belong. No more unnecessary cleanup. Just fast, clean, efficient servicing.

Every CVease is precision CNC-machined from 6061 aluminum, anodized in its signature model-specific color, and built to withstand a lifetime of use. Whether you're a racer, fabricator, off-road enthusiast, or restoration specialist, CVease helps you spend less time cleaning up and more time doing what you actually enjoy.

Because servicing your CV joints shouldn't be the messiest part of your build.
